methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $530 total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

data.narrative:
MakinaRocks is a Series B-stage AI & Software company founded by Sangmin Yoon that has raised 530 million KRW to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of nine private companies and one public reference point (PLTR), with five primary peers in the same sector and stage: Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the 530 million KRW total raised as a proxy base value and applies a typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x, implying a valuation of approximately $1.0B. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $5.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $32.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in market positioning and funding momentum within frontier AI.

Triangulating these methods produces an implied range of $856M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.5B. The stage-progression method anchors the conservative end, while peer multiples dominate the upper bound. Key uncertainties include the degree to which MakinaRocks' technology differentiation, go-to-market traction, and capital efficiency track toward the upper or lower quartile of the peer set, as well as broader market appetite for Series B AI funding in the current environment.
